name: Kitwood
overview: Kitwood is a small, dispersed rural hamlet in the parish of Four Marks (historically part of Ropley) in East Hampshire, on high downland between Ropley and Four Marks. It is an ancient place - Kitwood Lane is recorded in a 1413 court roll as 'kitteswodeweye', and hedgerows along it have been dated at roughly 800 years old - but it has always been a scattering of farms and cottages rather than a nucleated village.
character: A tiny, scattered downland hamlet of farmsteads and cottages along old lanes, deep in arable and wooded countryside.
amenities: Old Down Wood and a network of ancient lanes and footpaths nearby
Shops, schools and pubs in neighbouring Four Marks and Ropley
No shop, pub or school within Kitwood itself

commutes.notes: Nearest mainline station, about 4 to 5 miles east, on the Alton line to London Waterloo
For the wider range of city shops, services and fast trains
From Alton on South Western Railway; the heritage Watercress Line stops at nearby Medstead & Four Marks

prices.summary: A very small market of period farmhouses, cottages and occasional larger country houses, with very low transaction volumes; the secluded rural setting near sought-after Four Marks supports strong values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking the ancient lanes and Old Down Wood around the hamlet
Riding the heritage Watercress Line from nearby Medstead & Four Marks
Exploring Ropley, Four Marks and the wider East Hampshire countryside
placesToEat: Pubs and eateries in neighbouring Ropley and Four Marks
pros: Amenities and a primary school close by in Four Marks
Within a few miles of Alton for trains to London
cons: No shop, pub or school in the hamlet itself
Car essential; very limited public transport
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking a secluded, historic rural hamlet with the everyday amenities of Four Marks and Alton's rail link a short drive away.

sources.label: Ofsted - Four Marks CE Primary School
